{{Quote|Giju stew? It looks like old boot plastic and fertilizer drenched in pond scum. Smells like it, too—|Luke Skywalker|<ref name="SOTE" />}}
'''Giju stew''' was a dish with a foul odor that was best made with a bit of [[Boontaspice/Legends|Boontaspice]]. [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] encountered [[Giju/Legends|Giju]] [[Stew/Legends|stew]] from his youth on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], when his Aunt [[Beru Whitesun Lars/Legends|Beru Lars]] used to make it, to his [[Standard day/Legends|days]] fighting with the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] and later the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. In one instance, [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]] made a vain attempt at serving Giju stew to a repulsed Skywalker and [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] after using too much Boontaspice in his recipe.

==Description==
{{Quote|Come on, I spent an hour in the galley fixing this. Everybody dig in!|Lando Calrissian|<ref name="SOTE" />}}
Giju stew was a foul-smelling [[Stew/Legends|stew]] that was best made with a bit of [[Boontaspice/Legends|Boontaspice]], although not even that could mask the dish's repellent odor.<ref name="CSWE-giju stew" /> The stew was edible to members of the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Species/Legends|species]]<ref name="SOTE" /><ref name="Destiny's Way" /> and shared its name<ref name="CSWE-giju stew" /> with [[Giju/Legends|Giju]], the [[Herglic/Legends|Herglic]] [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]].<ref name="CSWE-Giju">{{CSWECite|I|340|Giju}}</ref>

==History==
{{Quote|Fine, fine! Don't eat it, that'll just mean more for me. See? It tastes great, it— Oh, man. Maybe I did use a little too much Boontaspice.|Lando Calrissian|<ref name="SOTE" />}}
[[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]] of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] once served Giju stew to his friends [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]], and the [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]] while aboard the [[YT-1300 light freighter/Legends|starship]] [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']]<ref name="SOTE" /> in [[3 ABY/Legends|3.5 ABY]].<ref name="Date">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates the events of the [[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire (novel)|''Shadows of the Empire'' novel]] to [[3 ABY/Legends|3.5 ABY]].</ref> Although Calrissian spent an [[Standard hour/Legends|hour]] preparing the meal in the [[Starship/Legends|ship's]] galley, his friends refused to touch his concoction, which both Skywalker and Organa thought resembled a cross between melted [[Boot/Legends|boot]] [[Plastic/Legends|plastic]] and [[Fertilizer/Legends|fertilizer]] sprinkled with pond scum. Proud of his creation, Calrissian sampled the stew himself but found to his disgust that he had used too much Boontaspice in his recipe. He then scrapped the meal and decided to eat something else.<ref name="SOTE" />

In [[26 ABY/Legends|26 ABY]],<ref name="Essential Reader's Companion">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]''</ref> during the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]], Skywalker and [[Marriage/Legends|wife]] [[Mara Jade Skywalker]] took a brief respite from their struggles on an island [[Beach/Legends|beach]]. As Skywalker walked barefoot through the beach's sand, memories of his youth on the [[Desert/Legends|desert]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] filled his thoughts, and he could nearly smell the Giju stew that his Aunt [[Beru Whitesun Lars/Legends|Beru Lars]] used to make.<ref name="Rebirth">''[[The New Jedi Order: Edge of Victory II: Rebirth]]''</ref> Later during the war, in [[28 ABY/Legends|28 ABY]],<ref name="Essential Reader's Companion" /> Skywalker consumed a bowl of Giju stew in the presence of his wife and [[Cal Omas]] on the New Republic [[Fleet Command (space station)|Fleet Command]] [[Space station/Legends|space station]], shortly before Omas's [[New Republic Presidential election, 28 ABY|election]] to New Republic [[Chief of State (New Republic)/Legends|Chief of State]].<ref name="Destiny's Way" />

==Behind the scenes==
Giju stew first appeared in the [[May]] [[1996]] novel [[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire (novel)|''Shadows of the Empire'']], written by [[Steve Perry]].<ref name="SOTE" /> It was later featured in two novels of [[Star Wars: The New Jedi Order|''The New Jedi Order'']] series, [[The New Jedi Order: Edge of Victory II: Rebirth|''Edge of Victory II: Rebirth'']] by [[Greg Keyes]] ([[2001]])<ref name="Rebirth" /> and [[The New Jedi Order: Destiny's Way|''Destiny's Way'']] by [[Walter Jon Williams]] ([[2002]]).<ref name="Destiny's Way" />

The stew's name references Giju, the home planet of the Herglic species, which originated in the [[1992]] ''[[Dark Force Rising Sourcebook]]'', a ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' supplement.<ref name="Dark Force Rising">''[[Dark Force Rising Sourcebook]]''</ref> However, while "Giju stew" is capitalized in both ''Shadows of the Empire''<ref name="SOTE" /> and the [[1998]] ''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'',<ref name="SWE">''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' {{C|"Giju stew"}}</ref> its name is formatted lower case as "giju stew" in the aforementioned ''The New Jedi Order'' novels<ref name="Destiny's Way" /><ref name="Rebirth" /> as well as the [[2008]] ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''.<ref name="CSWE-giju stew" /> Nevertheless, as "Giju" is a proper noun in relation to the planet name,<ref name="CSWE-Giju" /> this article uses the capitalized format.
